items.title: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
items.summary: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

items.title: Nuclear-Arms Exposure in ESG Funds Rises 95%
items.summary:
Bloomberg Intelligence's Shaheen Contractor joins Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ."

ESG and sustainable funds' saw 95% increase in exposure to nuclear stocks since June 2025. The increase in exposure was likely aided by EU guidance last year that left nuclear arms out of its controversial-weapons definition. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Lukashenko says Belarus will not join Putin’s war in Ukraine
items.summary: Kyiv feared that Moscow was pressuring its closest ally to support war effort

items.title: The UK could help end Sudan’s civil war
items.summary: The British government has leverage and should use it to proscribe a terrorist militia and cut off its supply lines

items.title: Zelenskyy says ‘battle in the sky’ will decide outcome of war with Russia
items.summary: Ukrainian leader says Donald Trump has changed his mind about the conflict

items.title: Judge rules insurers do not need to pay out over Nord Stream pipeline blasts
items.summary: Lloyd’s of London and others spared potential €580mn payout as court finds ‘war exclusion’ applies for Russia’s busted pipes

items.title: Peace Deal Doesn’t Restore Pre-War Situation, ECB’s Schnabel Says
items.summary: Peace efforts in the Middle East have helped bring energy prices down rapidly but haven’t restored the position the world was in before hostilities erupted, according to European Central Bank Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

items.title: Bank of Canada Surveys Show War Boosted Inflation Expectations, Investment
items.summary: The war in Iran caused a spike in Canadian inflation expectations and is leading the country’s oil producers to boost their investment and production plans.

items.title: Saudis Slash Main Oil Price to Rare Discount
items.summary: Ellen Wald, president of Transversal Consulting and Author of 'Saudi, Inc.,' said that Saudi state oil producer Saudi Aramco is lowering the price of oil to make it worthwhile for Asian buyers to charter a tanker to go into the Strait of Hormuz and to buy up stores of oil that have laid stagnant during the war.
